channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼 US Arms Sales to Taiwan Unrelated to Iran War, Source Says
item.description: A source familiar with the matter clarified that US arms sales to Taiwan take years to process and are unrelated to the ongoing conflict with Iran, following a senior US official suggesting there was a pause due to needing weapons for the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇳 Rubio Arrives in India to Repair Strained US Ties
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday to shore up a partnership battered by President Trump's tariffs and Washington's renewed engagement with India's rivals Pakistan and China.

item.title: 🇨🇭🇪🇺⚔️ Switzerland Expands Sanctions Against Russia and Belarus
item.description: Switzerland adopted parts of the European Union's latest sanctions package against Russia and Belarus over Moscow's war in Ukraine, expanding its sanctions lists in line with EU measures.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Considered Leaving OPEC for Three Years Before Exit
item.description: The UAE's decision to leave OPEC was three years in the making and based on its view the world is near the "autumn of the hydrocarbon age", meaning the country needs to maximise oil revenues while it can, a senior presidential adviser said.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump Official Pushed Plan to Ban Half of US Voting Machines
item.description: White House adviser Kurt Olsen, a lawyer tasked with proving election-rigging conspiracy theories, pushed a plan to target Dominion Voting Systems machines, according to a Reuters report.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Analysis: Three months in, is Trump losing the Iran war?
item.description: Despite winning nearly every battle against Iran, Trump faces growing doubts about whether he can translate tactical successes into a geopolitical win, with Iran still controlling the Strait of Hormuz and resisting nuclear concessions.

item.title: 🛒 Shein to buy apparel retailer Everlane
item.description: Fast-fashion giant Shein announced an agreement to acquire US apparel brand Everlane, known for sustainability and supply-chain transparency, marking a significant expansion into Western retail.

item.title: 🇮🇩🇷🇺 Indonesia working on scheme to import 150M barrels of Russian oil
item.description: Indonesia is developing a special import framework to fulfill its plan to import 150 million barrels of Russian crude this year, though only one shipment has reached the country so far.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Ukraine attacks Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, Zelenskiy says
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck a Russian oil refinery 700 km from the Ukrainian border, the latest in Kyiv's ongoing campaign targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi calls for all-out rescue after coal mine explosion kills over 80 in China
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping demanded an investigation and accountability after a gas explosion at a Shanxi Province coal mine killed at least 82 people, with rescue operations ongoing.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Green Card Applicants Must Return Home First Under New Trump Policy
item.description: US Citizenship and Immigration Services announced that foreigners seeking permanent residency must now apply from their home countries, ending a provision that allowed applications from within the US. The policy could affect hundreds of thousands of applicants annually.

item.title: 🇦🇪 Israel Exposes UAE Defense Ties With Iron Dome Deployment Reveal
item.description: US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee inadvertently exposed UAE-Israel defense cooperation by confirming Iron Dome battery deployments. The disclosure puts Abu Dhabi in an awkward diplomatic position after months of denying Iranian accusations of military collaboration.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Faces Mixed Signals as Trump Warning Meets Historic Arms Offer
item.description: A proposed billion arms package discussion between Washington and Taipei collides with Trump statement that he is not looking for anyone to go independent, creating policy whiplash for the island amid heightened cross-strait tensions.

item.title: 🇸🇮 Slovenia parliament approves right-wing Janez Jansa as prime minister
item.description: Slovenia's parliament approved the return of populist former PM Janez Jansa, marking a sharp rightward shift for the EU member state previously governed by liberals.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France presidential race sees ex-PM Philippe as early favorite to beat populists
item.description: Latest polls suggest center-right figure Philippe is the only candidate who can defeat Marine Le Pen or Jean-Luc Melenchon in Frances next presidential election.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US tells temporary visa holders to leave country to apply for green cards
item.description: New policy closes loophole allowing people to apply for permanent residency while in the US, forcing foreigners to apply from abroad.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta province plans referendum vote on leaving Canada
item.description: Alberta officials announced plans for a public vote on whether to hold a binding referendum on separation from Canada. The move has drawn criticism from federalists and some separatists who question the wording.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
item.description: Russia President Putin promised retaliation after Ukraine allegedly struck a student dormitory. Ukraine said it targeted Russias elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied part of eastern Ukraine.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz with new map
item.description: Iran has published a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 square kilometers of the strategic waterway, escalating tensions in the region as ceasefire negotiations continue.

item.title: 🇲🇽🇪🇺 Mexico and EU Launch Modernized Trade Agreement
item.description: Mexico and the European Union celebrated a modernized global agreement aimed at reducing tariffs and strengthening bilateral trade relations, diversifying away from US dependency.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚔️ UK Simulates AI-Driven NATO War Games Against Russia
item.description: The British army conducted large-scale underground wargames simulating a 2030 NATO conflict with Russia, testing AI-driven battlefield decision-making systems beneath central London.

item.title: ⚛️ UN Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Fails Amid US-Iran Clash
item.description: The four-week NPT review conference ended without consensus for the third consecutive time, as US and Iran clashed over Tehran's nuclear program during negotiations.

item.title: 🏦 World Bank: 27 Countries Seek Crisis Funding Access Amid Iran War
item.description: Twenty-seven countries have moved to establish crisis instruments for rapid access to World Bank funding since the Iran war began, according to an internal document. The move reflects growing global economic strain from the conflict.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Police: Prince Andrew Investigation Will Be Lengthy
item.description: British police said their investigation into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will be long and complex, after his arrest earlier this year on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The case involves King Charles' brother.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Rubio Sees Progress in Iran Talks, Says More Work Needed
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reported progress in US-Iran peace negotiations but noted sticking points remain over Iran's enriched uranium and control of the Strait of Hormuz.
